creator: petefrench@ticketswitch.com
text:
> Actually, this is not the only port that doesn't install a static library.
> I'd say the only reason a lot of ports install them is that they're using
> libtool which is hard to prevent installing static libraries along with
> infamous .la files.

Ah, OK, didn't realise that - so are static libraries effectively discouraged
in ports then ? I have never come across one which didn't, but (as you say)
most of them use libtool.

> The reason we do it is exactly to prevent static linking to happen.  Imagine
> the pretty common situation when some kind of security vulnerability is
> discovered in libdispatch: in that case we'll fix the vulnerability and

That makes sense - I never worry about things like that because
I always update all ports en-mass and relink everything. But I
realise other people dont - it's easy in my case because it's just one
machine, and then I ship static executable out to the farm. Ironicly
moving to shared libraryes will make it far less likely for security
fixes to get deployed, as I would have to go and update the port
on every individual machine.

> I understand, that static linking might be desirable for some
> commercial applications.  One of solutions I could suggest is
> to install the static version of the library in some special
> folder when you will be able to link agains it by providing
> the path to it explicitly.  This way we'll be able to prevent
> accidential static linking from happening.

A kind of /usr/lib/static and /usr/local/lib/static ? Yes, that sounds
like an excellent idea. I think it would need to be standardised
somehow, as if every port did it's own thing, then five years down
the line you have libraryes scattered all over the place depending
on where the port author decided to put them.

Out of interest - why do you still ship the base system with
static libraries ? Surely if you just got rid of libc.a  then
nobody is ever going to end up with an accidentally static linked
executable as it simply wont run ?

Anyway, thanks for the explanation - would you like me to have a go
at making a patch for libdespatch to place statics into /usr/local/lib/static?
